# Speci

> AI-powered implementation loop orchestrator for GitHub Copilot. Automate plan → task → implement → review workflows with quality gate validation.

Speci is a Node.js CLI tool (requires Node.js ≥ 22) that orchestrates GitHub Copilot agents in an autonomous development loop. Given a prompt or specification, it generates an implementation plan, breaks it into discrete tasks tracked in PROGRESS.md, and then iterates: dispatching an implementation agent, running configured quality gates (lint, typecheck, tests), and automatically fixing failures — all without manual intervention.

The loop state machine has five states: WORK_LEFT (impl agent runs), IN_REVIEW (review agent evaluates), BLOCKED (tidy agent unblocks), DONE (loop exits cleanly), and NO_PROGRESS (no PROGRESS.md found, run `speci task` first).

---

## Getting Started

URL: /getting-started/

### Prerequisites

- Node.js >= 22
- GitHub Copilot CLI installed and authenticated (npm install -g @github/copilot)
- A Git repository

### Install

One-off with npx (no install needed):
  npx speci init

Or install globally:
  npm install -g speci

### Initialise your project

  npx speci init

This creates a speci.config.json in your project root, creates docs/tasks/ and .speci-logs/ directories, and copies the built-in agent templates into .github/agents/.

On first run, speci fetches the live Copilot model list and asks you to choose a preset (best, balanced, budget) or configure each agent role individually. In non-interactive environments the Balanced preset is applied automatically.

  # Apply a preset non-interactively (e.g. in CI)
  npx speci init --preset balanced

  # Configure models one-by-one
  npx speci init --custom

  # Update models in an existing config
  npx speci init --reconfigure-models

### Run your first workflow

  # 1. Generate a plan from a prompt
  npx speci plan -p "Add user authentication with JWT" --output docs/plan.md

  # 2. Break the plan into tasks
  npx speci task --plan docs/plan.md

  # 3. Start the implementation loop
  npx speci run

### One-shot (yolo) mode

  # Plan -> task -> run in a single command
  npx speci yolo -p "Add user authentication with JWT"

### Configuration

Speci reads speci.config.json in your project root. A minimal config:

  {
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "gate": {
      "commands": ["npm run lint", "npm run typecheck", "npm test"],
      "maxFixAttempts": 5,
      "strategy": "sequential"
    }
  }

---

## Commands Overview

URL: /commands/

| Command          | Description                                              |
| speci init       | Initialise speci in your project                         |
| speci plan       | Generate a structured implementation plan via Copilot    |
| speci task       | Break a plan into trackable tasks and create PROGRESS.md |
| speci run        | Start the autonomous implementation loop                 |
| speci yolo       | Full pipeline: plan -> task -> run                       |
| speci refactor   | Analyse the codebase for refactoring opportunities       |
| speci status     | Print the current loop state                             |
| speci clean      | Remove generated task files and PROGRESS.md              |

---

## speci init

URL: /commands/init/

Initialise speci in an existing project. Creates speci.config.json, sets up copilot.models from the live Copilot model list, and copies the built-in agent templates into .github/agents/.

Usage:
  speci init [-u] [-v] [--preset <best|balanced|budget>] [--custom] [--reconfigure-models]

Options:
  -u, --update-agents       Update agent files even if they already exist
  --preset <preset>         Model preset: best, balanced, or budget (pre-selects menu default)
  --custom                  Configure copilot.models role-by-role from the live model list
  --reconfigure-models      Update copilot.models in an existing speci.config.json
  -v, --verbose             Show detailed output

Model selection:
On first init, speci fetches the live Copilot model list and presents a menu:

  ? Choose a model preset:
    1. Best-in-Class
    2. Balanced
    3. Budget-Friendly
    4. Custom (one-by-one)

Preset assigns the best available model to each role automatically.
Custom prompts you to pick a model for each of the seven roles: plan, task, refactor, impl, review, fix, and tidy.
Passing --preset pre-selects the matching menu option; in non-interactive (CI) environments the preset is applied directly.
Use --reconfigure-models to revisit model selection on an existing config; --preset then applies immediately.

What it creates:
  speci.config.json                          Project configuration (gates, agent paths, copilot.models, etc.)
  docs/tasks/                                Directory for generated task files
  .speci-logs/                               Directory for run log files
  .github/agents/speci-plan.agent.md         Plan generation agent
  .github/agents/speci-impl.agent.md         Implementation agent
  .github/agents/speci-review.agent.md       Review agent
  .github/agents/speci-fix.agent.md          Gate failure fix agent
  .github/agents/speci-task.agent.md         Task generation agent
  .github/agents/speci-tidy.agent.md         Blocked-state tidy agent
  .github/agents/speci-refactor.agent.md     Refactor analysis agent
  .github/agents/subagents/                  33 multi-pass subagent prompts

---

## speci plan

URL: /commands/plan/

Interactive plan generation via GitHub Copilot CLI.

Usage:
  speci plan [-p "..."] [-i <files...>] [-o <path>] [--sleep-after] [-v]

Options:
  -p, --prompt <text>     Free-text prompt describing the feature or change
  -i, --input <files...>  One or more input files (spec, design docs, etc.)
  -o, --output <path>     Path to write the generated plan (streams to terminal if omitted)
  --sleep-after           Put machine to sleep after command completes
  -v, --verbose           Show the full prompt and agent output

At least one of --prompt or --input is required.

Flow:
  1. Validate inputs — ensures --prompt or --input is provided and that input files exist.
  2. Initialise — loads speci.config.json, runs preflight checks (Copilot CLI available?), resolves the speci-plan agent.
  3. Build prompt — combines input file references, the --prompt text, and the --output path hint.
  4. Dispatch agent — runs the speci-plan.agent.md via Copilot CLI and streams output.
  5. Write plan — if --output is specified, saves the plan to that path; otherwise the agent streams output to the terminal.

Example:
  speci plan -p "Add OAuth2 login with GitHub" --output docs/auth-plan.md

Next: run speci task to break the plan into tasks.

---

## speci task

URL: /commands/task/

One-shot task generation from a plan file via GitHub Copilot CLI.

Usage:
  speci task -p <path> [-c] [--sleep-after] [-v]

Options:
  -p, --plan <path>   Path to the plan file generated by speci plan (required)
  -c, --clean         Remove existing task files and PROGRESS.md before generating
  --sleep-after       Put machine to sleep after command completes
  -v, --verbose       Show the full prompt and agent output

Flow:
  1. Validate --plan — the flag is required; exits with an error if missing.
  2. Resolve path — resolves the plan file relative to the working directory.
  3. Validate file — checks the plan file exists and is readable.
  4. Clean (optional) — if --clean is set, removes any existing task files and PROGRESS.md.
  5. Initialise — loads config, runs preflight, resolves the speci-task agent.
  6. Dispatch agent — runs speci-task.agent.md with the plan file context.
  7. Write output — agent writes individual task files and a PROGRESS.md tracking file.

Example:
  speci task --plan docs/auth-plan.md

Next: run speci run to start the implementation loop.

---

## speci run

URL: /commands/run/

Central orchestration loop. Reads PROGRESS.md state, dispatches agents, runs gate validations, and iterates until all tasks are done or limits are reached.

Usage:
  speci run [--max-iterations 100] [--dry-run] [--force] [-y] [--verify] [--sleep-after] [-v]

Options:
  --max-iterations <n>   Maximum number of loop iterations (default: 100)
  --dry-run              Print what would happen without executing anything
  --force                Override existing lock
  -y, --yes              Skip confirmation prompt
  --verify               Pause on manual verification tasks (MVTs) at milestone boundaries
  --sleep-after          Put machine to sleep after command completes
  -v, --verbose          Show full agent output

Loop states:
  WORK_LEFT     impl agent dispatched — tasks remain; implementation agent runs next
  IN_REVIEW     review agent dispatched — tasks awaiting review; review agent evaluates implementation
  BLOCKED       tidy agent dispatched — all remaining tasks are blocked
  DONE          no agent — all tasks complete; loop exits
  NO_PROGRESS   no agent — no PROGRESS.md found; run speci task

Gate validation:
After every impl agent run, speci executes the gates defined in speci.config.json:
  {
    "gate": {
      "commands": ["npm run lint", "npm run typecheck", "npm test"],
      "maxFixAttempts": 5,
      "strategy": "sequential"
    }
  }
If any gate fails, the fix agent is dispatched (up to maxFixAttempts times). Gates are re-run after each fix attempt.

High-level flow:
  Load config -> Preflight -> Acquire lock
    MAIN LOOP (up to maxIterations)
      Read PROGRESS.md -> Dispatch agent
      If WORK_LEFT: impl -> gates -> (fix loop on failure)
      If IN_REVIEW:  review
      If BLOCKED:    tidy
      If DONE:       exit 0
    Cleanup (release lock, close logs)

Example:
  speci run --max-iterations 20 --yes

---

## speci yolo

URL: /commands/yolo/

Full pipeline orchestrator: plan -> task -> run in a single command. Chains all three commands sequentially with shared config and lock management.

Usage:
  speci yolo [-p "..."] [-i <files...>] [-o <path>] [--force] [--sleep-after] [-v]

Options:
  -p, --prompt <text>     Free-text prompt (passed to speci plan)
  -i, --input <files...>  Input files (passed to speci plan)
  -o, --output <path>     Plan output path (default: auto-generated timestamped path under docs/)
  --force                 Override existing lock
  --sleep-after           Put machine to sleep after command completes
  -v, --verbose           Show full agent output

At least one of --prompt or --input is required.

Pipeline:
  Phase 1/3: plan   — generate structured plan document
  Phase 2/3: task   — generate tasks and PROGRESS.md
  Phase 3/3: run    — autonomous implementation loop

Each phase must succeed before the next starts. A failure in any phase aborts the pipeline with a phase-aware error message.

Example:
  speci yolo -p "Add OAuth2 login with GitHub" --force

This is equivalent to running:
  speci plan -p "Add OAuth2 login with GitHub" --output <timestamped-path>
  speci task -p <timestamped-path>
  speci run --yes --force

---

## speci refactor

URL: /commands/refactor/

One-shot codebase refactoring analysis via GitHub Copilot CLI. Dispatches the speci-refactor agent to identify and document refactoring opportunities.

Usage:
  speci refactor [-s <path-or-glob>] [-o <path>] [-v]

Options:
  -s, --scope <path-or-glob>   Limit analysis to a path or glob pattern (e.g. src/utils, lib/**)
  -o, --output <path>          Path to write the refactoring analysis
  -v, --verbose                Show the full prompt and agent output

Flow:
  1. Scope validation — if --scope is a path (not a glob), resolves it and warns if it doesn't exist (non-fatal). Glob patterns are passed as-is.
  2. Initialise — loads config, runs preflight, resolves the speci-refactor agent.
  3. Dispatch agent — runs speci-refactor.agent.md with the scope context.
  4. Write output — saves the refactoring analysis to the output file.

Example:
  speci refactor --scope src/api --output docs/api-refactor.md

---

## speci status

URL: /commands/status/

Displays the current loop state and task statistics as a live fullscreen dashboard. Press q to quit the dashboard.

Usage:
  speci status [--json] [--once] [-v]

Options:
  --json       Output status as JSON and exit
  --once       Show status once and exit (non-interactive)
  -v, --verbose  Show detailed status

Output:
By default opens a live fullscreen dashboard that refreshes automatically (press q to quit). Use --once for a single-shot summary or --json to emit machine-readable output.

Task states: NOT STARTED, IN PROGRESS, IN REVIEW, BLOCKED, COMPLETE
Loop states: WORK_LEFT, IN_REVIEW, BLOCKED, DONE, NO_PROGRESS

---

## speci clean

URL: /commands/clean/

Removes generated files created by speci task and speci run so you can start fresh.

Usage:
  speci clean [-y] [-v]

Options:
  -y, --yes      Skip confirmation prompt
  -v, --verbose  Show which files are being removed

What it removes:
  docs/tasks/ (contents)   All generated task files
  docs/PROGRESS.md         Task tracking file

Note: speci clean refuses to run while another speci process holds the lock. Wait for the active run to finish first.
